subjectLine: Claude just got scary good (and it's free)
weekTopic: The AI Arms Race Heats Up

bigStory.headline: Anthropic Made Claude Sonnet 4.6 Free for Everyone
bigStory.summary: Anthropic just dropped Claude Sonnet 4.6 as the default for all users, free and paid. This isn't some watered-down version. It handles coding, long documents, research, and can literally fill out web forms and work across browser tabs. The gap between free and paid AI is basically gone.

classConnection: If you're still paying $20/month for ChatGPT Plus to do your FME business plan research, stop. Claude's free tier now does 90% of what you need. Save that money for customer acquisition.

threeThings.headline: xAI Built a 4-Agent Team That Actually Works
threeThings.summary: Grok 4.20 uses four specialized agents on every query. One coordinates, one fact-checks, one handles math and code, one does creative work. Early tests show hallucinations dropped 47-65%.
threeThings.takeaway: This is where AI is heading. Single models are out, agent teams are in. Start thinking about your startup problems as tasks you'd delegate to a team, not questions for one assistant.

threeThings.headline: ElevenLabs Hit $11B Valuation on Voice AI
threeThings.summary: The voice cloning startup raised $500M at an $11B valuation. Sequoia led. They went from zero to unicorn in under three years.
threeThings.takeaway: Voice interfaces are the next platform shift. If your app still requires typing, you're building for 2023.

threeThings.headline: OpenAI Launched Frontier for Enterprise Agents
threeThings.summary: Companies can now build and deploy AI agents inside their existing infrastructure. Connects to third-party systems and handles complex workflows without custom engineering.
threeThings.takeaway: Enterprise AI agent tools are commoditizing fast. The opportunity isn't building another agent platform. It's being the expert who deploys these for specific industries.

founderFramework.title: Validate your startup idea this weekend
founderFramework.intro: You don't need weeks of research. Here's the exact process, with prompts you can copy right now.

steps.title: Map your competition in 30 minutes
steps.description: Get a full competitive landscape without hours of Googling.
steps.prompt:
I'm building [DESCRIBE YOUR STARTUP IDEA IN ONE SENTENCE].

Find the top 10 companies solving this problem. For each one, give me:
1. Company name and website
2. Their pricing model
3. Who their target customer is
4. Their biggest weakness based on user reviews
5. What makes them different from the others

Format as a table I can reference.

steps.title: Find real customer pain on Reddit
steps.description: Discover exactly how people describe their frustration. This becomes your landing page copy.
steps.prompt:
Search Reddit for complaints about [YOUR PROBLEM SPACE]. Find 15-20 posts where people are frustrated with current solutions.

For each post, extract:
1. The exact phrase they used to describe their frustration
2. What they wish existed
3. How much they said they'd pay to fix it (if mentioned)

Group the complaints by theme.

steps.title: Write your landing page and test it
steps.description: Turn those Reddit pain points into copy that converts.
steps.prompt:
I'm launching [YOUR STARTUP NAME] which solves [PROBLEM].

Here are real complaints from potential customers:
[PASTE YOUR REDDIT FINDINGS]

Write landing page copy that:
1. Opens with the exact frustration they described
2. Positions my solution as the obvious fix
3. Includes 3 benefit bullets using their language
4. Ends with a clear CTA to join the waitlist

Keep it under 200 words. No corporate speak.

founderFramework.bottomLine: Total cost: $50 in Google Ads and one weekend. You'll know more than founders who spent three months on market research.

aiInBusiness.title: Three Workflows You Can Set Up Today

workflows.title: Investor meeting prep
workflows.description: Stop going into meetings blind. Get personalized talking points in 5 minutes.
workflows.prompt:
I have a meeting with [INVESTOR NAME] from [FIRM NAME].

Their LinkedIn: [PASTE LINKEDIN URL OR SUMMARY]
Their recent investments: [LIST ANY YOU KNOW]

Give me:
1. Three talking points specific to their investment thesis
2. Two questions that show I did my research
3. One thing to definitely avoid mentioning
4. How to connect my startup to their portfolio

workflows.title: Cold email that doesn't suck
workflows.description: Write outreach that actually gets responses.
workflows.prompt:
I need to email [PERSON'S NAME AND ROLE] at [COMPANY].

Context about them: [PASTE ANY INFO YOU HAVE]
What I want: [YOUR ASK - meeting, intro, feedback, etc]
Why they should care: [WHAT'S IN IT FOR THEM]

Write a cold email that:
- Subject line under 5 words
- Opening line that's NOT 'I hope this finds you well'
- Gets to the ask in under 100 words
- Sounds like a human, not a template

workflows.title: Weekly competitor monitoring
workflows.description: Know what your competitors are doing before your investors ask.
workflows.prompt:
Search for news about these companies from the past 7 days:
[COMPETITOR 1]
[COMPETITOR 2]
[COMPETITOR 3]

For each, find:
1. Any product launches or updates
2. Funding announcements
3. New hires (especially executives)
4. Press coverage or interviews

Summarize what I need to know as a competitor.

quickHits: Apple partnering with Google to rebuild Siri using Gemini. Paying $1B/year for it.
OpenAI testing ads in free ChatGPT. Paid tiers stay ad-free for now.
Adobe killing Animate on March 1. Shifting everything to AI products.
Meta embedded AI agents directly into Ads Manager. Handles testing and optimization automatically.
nextWeek: Apple's March 4 event is dropping at least five products. I'll break down what actually matters for founders and what's just consumer noise.
